question: Looking at the US population map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Iowa and Minnesota have similar populations.
options.B: The population gap between Iowa and Minnesota might exceed that between Kansas and South Dakota.
options.C: All highlighted states have nearly identical populations.
options.D: The population differences between the highlighted states are impossible to judge from this map.

explanation: Iowa (20.8M) and Minnesota (29.2M) have a 8.4M population difference, while Kansas (29.5M) and South Dakota (30.4M) have only a 0.9M difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Iowa and Minnesota into the same color bin (20-30M range), making their large difference appear similar, while Kansas and South Dakota appear more different due to different color bins.
